intro: Building or renovating requires precise calculations: material quantities, concrete dosing, yields per square meter, unit costs. Values are current through 2026 and updated regularly to keep you informed.

explanation:
**Building construction** requires technical calculations: concrete, steel, bricks, plaster, waterproofing. IRAM and CAC standardize processes in Argentina.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator determines the number of bricks needed based on your brick type and wall area in square meters. It applies the standard formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you if values seem unreasonable.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a guide only. For critical decisions (financial, structural, legal), consult a licensed professional. Values are current through 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How many bricks per square meter?
faq.a: Common brick: ~60 per m². Load-bearing: ~40 per m². Cavity wall: ~50 per m². Varies by thickness and joint width.

faq.q: What's the difference between common and load-bearing bricks?
faq.a: Load-bearing bricks are structural and support building weight. Common bricks are for non-structural walls and partitions.

faq.q: What percentage extra should I order for waste?
faq.a: Add 5-10% extra for cuts, breakage, and installation losses. On large projects, this adds up quickly.

faq.q: How much mortar do I need per square meter?
faq.a: Common brick: ~0.06 m³/m². Load-bearing: ~0.04 m³/m². Made from sand and cement; mix ratios vary.

faq.q: What's the weight of a brick wall?
faq.a: Common brick weighs ~1.3 kg each. A 15 cm thick wall: ~150 kg/m². Factor this into structural planning.

faq.q: What are alternative materials to bricks?
faq.a: Concrete block (faster installation). Thermal clay blocks (insulation). Exposed brick (aesthetic finish). Each has different costs and benefits.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes. All our calculators are free with no registration required.
Results are estimates based on standard formulas validated internationally. For critical applications, consult a structural engineer.
No. Calculations run in your browser. We don't send data to any server.
